intents.name: greeting
responses: Hi — I'm your posture assistant. Tell me about your neck or shoulder pain and I'll try to help.
Hello! I can help with neck, shoulder and upper-back posture issues. What are you feeling?

intents.name: neck_pain
responses: For neck pain related to posture: try gentle chin tucks, keep the screen at eye level, and take short breaks every 30 minutes.
Neck pain often comes from forward head posture. Straighten your neck, lower your shoulders, and try these stretches: look left-right, and gentle shoulder rolls.

intents.name: shoulder_pain
responses: Shoulder pain from slouching can improve with scapular squeezes — pull your shoulder blades together 10x every hour and strengthen mid-back muscles.
Try to avoid slumping; sit up, tuck your chin slightly, and do gentle doorway stretches for the chest to relieve shoulder tension.

intents.name: back_pain
responses: Upper-back discomfort is often postural. Use a lumbar-supported chair, sit tall, and perform mid-back extensions occasionally.
Focus on posture: roll shoulders back, open the chest, and take standing breaks to reduce back strain from slouching.

intents.name: posture_tips
responses: Keep your monitor at eye level, feet flat, shoulders relaxed, and take 2-minute posture breaks every 30 minutes.
Work on strengthening the deep neck flexors and mid-back; small daily habits (desk height, chair support) make a big difference.

intents.name: calibration_help
responses: To calibrate the device, sit in a neutral upright posture and press the Calibrate button in the app. The device will record this as the neutral reference.
Calibration helps remove sensor drift. Sit comfortably upright, keep your head level, and use the app's Calibrate function once.

intents.name: fallback
responses: I can help with neck, shoulder and upper-back posture and pain. Can you describe where it hurts or what you were doing when it started?
Sorry, I can only help with posture-related issues (neck, shoulder, upper back). Please ask about those topics.
